  "clarification_question": "To scope “Check this,” should I focus on: (A) logical/quantitative consistency and claim–evidence alignment, (B) writing polish/clarity only, (C) contribution framing/novelty positioning, or (D) a comprehensive review across all of the above? parsed.clarification_question: To scope “Check this,” should I focus on: (A) logical/quantitative consistency and claim–evidence alignment, (B) writing polish/clarity only, (C) contribution framing/novelty positioning, or (D) a comprehensive review across all of the above? Please reply A, B, C, or D.

rationale.R1.2: The text advances specific argument chains around routing policy and performance that need internal logical consistency checks.
rationale.R1.3: Quantitative claims (cost and latency) must be checked against the reported figures to ensure alignment.
rationale.RX.1: Given the ambiguous request and partial context, any assessment should clearly note uncertainties and limits.
rationale.RX.2: Claims such as Pareto improvement and deployment recommendations should be evaluated cautiously to avoid overstatement.
rationale.RX.3: The check must remain within reviewing the provided draft content without performing new experiments or external searches.
rationale.RX.4: Incorrect or overstated performance claims could mislead operators, so potential downstream impacts should be considered.
rationale.RX.5: All observations should be traceable to specific statements and numbers in the provided artifact.
